# Skill Manager

> Skill Manager is a local-first, free, open-source inventory and health check for Agent Skills installed for Codex, Claude, and shared agent environments.

Skill Manager scans supported global skill roots, records path and package provenance, matches local session evidence, estimates the catalog context formed by each skill name and description, and provides ledger-backed archive and restore workflows. Inventory, usage analysis, archive, restore, and report export run locally. The project uses the MIT License and has one free plan at $0 forever.

## Run the CLI

- Audit: `npx github:Ryan-yang125/skill-manager audit`
- Homebrew install: `brew install Ryan-yang125/skill-manager/skill-manager`
- JSON report: `npx github:Ryan-yang125/skill-manager audit --json`
- Markdown report: `npx github:Ryan-yang125/skill-manager audit --markdown`
- Inspect: `npx github:Ryan-yang125/skill-manager inspect <skill-id> --json`
- Archive preview: `npx github:Ryan-yang125/skill-manager archive <skill-id> --dry-run --json`
- Restore: `npx github:Ryan-yang125/skill-manager restore <archive-id> --yes --json`

## Install the Agent Skill

- `npx skills add Ryan-yang125/skill-manager --skill audit-agent-skills -g -y`

## Product facts

- Supported global roots: `~/.agents/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`, `~/.claude/skills`
- Supported desktop platforms: macOS, Windows, Linux
- Local session evidence: recent and archived Codex sessions plus Claude project logs when available
- Catalog context estimate: skill name and description only
- Archive safety: durable local ledger, exact original path, overwrite guard, restore workflow
- Telemetry: zero in the current public release
- Price: $0 forever
- License: MIT

## Important interpretation rule

Zero matched usage evidence describes the scanned local logs. It is a review signal. Session coverage may be incomplete, and rare skills can remain essential.
